statement: The system shall provide a baseline configuration that can be adapted to meet project-specific requirements such as longer arms, increased lift capacity, or waterproofing.
The system shall enter safe mode when it receives movement commands that exceed its programmed capabilities or that it cannot process at the requested speed.
The system shall display the position within the range for each joint and change its color when the joint approaches its limits.
The system shall allow a timeframe to be extended by 50% as a buffer.
The system shall provide stops or guides to keep the cable within designated boundaries.
The system shall allow programming of go‑to positions for the robot arm using teach files, enabling the arm to move automatically to specified points.
The system shall move the robot close to the target location and then transfer control to the operator for final positioning.
The system shall provide a variable viewpoint that allows users to look around to enhance depth perception.
The system shall provide fixed camera views, such as a goal line camera, that remain constant for their designated task.
The system shall provide cameras that automatically zoom in to follow the user.
The system shall prioritize image resolution over frame rate.
The system shall use force feedback to indicate when forward motion is required and stop movement if the user does not move forward.
The system shall provide a ghost mode that, when activated by a button press, displays the predicted final position.
The system shall provide an alarm when the user drifts toward a no‑go zone.
The system shall recognize materials such as rubber, aluminium, and painted surfaces through visual scanning.
The system shall feed material recognition into a no‑go zone and use coloured blocks as visual cues for instruction.
